text.title: Vector Databases — Failure Modes
text.summary: Five high-impact antipatterns when choosing or operating a vector DB.

failure-mode.description: Chroma in production multi-process — file lock contention, data corruption, weird intermittent failures.
failure-mode.detector: kind=="chroma" AND serve_mode=="multi-process" OR running under gunicorn/uvicorn workers >1
failure-mode.repair: Migrate to Qdrant or pgvector. Chroma is dev/notebook only.

failure-mode.description: Missing tenant filter — query without tenant_id returns docs from all tenants; data leak invisible in logs.
failure-mode.detector: retrieve() signature does not require tenant_id; production logs show queries without tenant filter
failure-mode.repair: Make tenant_id a required parameter at the function signature. Add a canary test: tenant A query MUST NOT return any tenant B doc id.

failure-mode.description: Embedding dim mismatch — switched models, collection still has old dim; ingest fails OR new vectors mis-stored.
failure-mode.detector: collection.dim differs from new model output dim
failure-mode.repair: Create new collection with correct dim; re-ingest; cut traffic over; drop old collection. Treat as major schema change.

failure-mode.description: Wrong distance metric — euclidean on OpenAI cosine embeddings; ranking is silently wrong.
failure-mode.repair: Recreate collection with correct metric. Document in vector-db.yaml mapping.

failure-mode.description: No backup / snapshot policy — DB corruption requires full re-embed from source ($$, time).
failure-mode.repair: Schedule daily Qdrant snapshot OR pg_dump for pgvector. Verify restore in staging quarterly.
